title: Feature Request: Allow for folders to set Hostname/IP
author: countchappy
body_snippet:
<!-- Feature Request -->
## Issue that prompted feature request?
Folders do not allow you to set a Hostname/IP for inheritance.

### Fix for specified issue:
- Allow folders to configure a Hostname/IP
- Allow for sub-folders/connections underneath the folder to inherit the setting

I can work on this myself and submit a PR if you would like

iterations.description: Clear feature request with multiple community votes to allow folders to define a shared Hostname/IP inherited by child connections, useful for multi-port/multi-user scenarios on the same host

comments.author: kmscode
comments.snippet:
@countchappy Could you provide some more details in regards to the use case?

The only thing that I can think of would be to have multiple connections with the same hostname/IP, but different credentials. Would like to understand this better.

comments.author: countchappy
comments.snippet:
@kmscode That is exactly what it would be for 😆
Currently we use mRemoteNG at my workplace to connect multiple users to our Terminal Server on a single workstation. So we have multiple users under a single folder for terminal server connections.
*(Image below for reference)*
![mremoteng_setup](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/23261143/23623692/f3965cc2-0270-11e7-8e26-2268968df9c2.JPG)
All of these connections connect to the same hostname `tserv.[redacted].com`
That's just an examp...

comments.author: PrestigeComputing
comments.snippet: I would also like this feature. I would use it exactly the same way as described above.

comments.author: ng3727
comments.snippet:
I would like this ability too e.g. if you have docker installed on a host with different ssh ports for different machines, the machine is the same but the port and/or usernames would be different.
For testing application users with different access this would be ideal linking to the same host but with different credentials.

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
**Fix available for testing**

A fix for this issue has been implemented in our community fork (`robertpopa22/mRemoteNG`, branch `main`). A beta build including this fix is available from the fork's Releases page.

Please test and report if this resolves your issue.

---

> *We sincerely appreciate the mRemoteNG maintainers and the wonderful community that has kept this project alive and thriving for over a decade. Your work powers thousands of IT professionals worldwide, including us. Our fork ...

notes:
AI triage: Clear feature request with multiple community votes to allow folders to define a shared Hostname/IP inherited by child connections, useful for multi-port/multi-user scenarios on the same host
Approach: Enable the Hostname property on folder/container nodes so child connections can inherit it, adding UI support in the config panel to set Hostname on folders and ensuring the inheritance resolver propagates it to children that don't override it
